concepts.name: Disjoint Sets
concepts.short_definition: A collection of sets where no element belongs to more than one set.

concepts.name: Union-Find Data Structure
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that tracks a set of elements partitioned into a number of disjoint subsets.

concepts.name: Find Operation
concepts.short_definition: Determines which subset a particular element is in. Returns a representative of that subset.

concepts.name: Union Operation
concepts.short_definition: Combines two subsets into a single subset.

concepts.name: Parent Array
concepts.short_definition: An array where each element stores the parent of that element in its set's tree representation.

concepts.name: Root Element
concepts.short_definition: The element in a set that is its own parent, serving as the identifier for the set.

concepts.name: Tree Representation
concepts.short_definition: Each disjoint set is represented as a tree, with the root being the representative.

concepts.name: Path Compression
concepts.short_definition: An optimization for the Find operation that flattens the tree by making every node on the path point directly to the root.

concepts.name: Union by Rank
concepts.short_definition: An optimization for the Union operation that attaches the shorter tree to the root of the taller tree.

concepts.name: Union by Size
concepts.short_definition: An optimization for the Union operation that attaches the smaller tree to the root of the larger tree.

concepts.name: Rank Array
concepts.short_definition: An array used with Union by Rank to store the rank (or height) of each tree.

concepts.name: Size Array
concepts.short_definition: An array used with Union by Size to store the size of each tree.

concepts.name: Amortized Analysis
concepts.short_definition: Analyzing the average performance of an operation over a sequence of operations.

concepts.name: Inverse Ackermann Function
concepts.short_definition: A function that grows extremely slowly, used to describe the near-constant time complexity of DSU operations with optimizations.

concepts.name: Kruskal's Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: A greedy algorithm to find a Minimum Spanning Tree (MST) for a connected, undirected graph.

concepts.name: Minimum Spanning Tree
concepts.short_definition: A subset of the edges of a connected, edge-weighted undirected graph that connects all the vertices together, without any cycles and with the minimum possible total edge weight.

concepts.name: Connected Components
concepts.short_definition: A subgraph in which any two vertices are connected to each other by paths, and which is connected to no additional vertices in the supergraph.

concepts.name: Cycle Detection
concepts.short_definition: Identifying if a graph contains any cycles.

concepts.name: Graph
concepts.short_definition: A data structure consisting of a set of vertices and a set of edges connecting pairs of vertices.

concepts.name: Edge
concepts.short_definition: A connection between two vertices in a graph.

concepts.name: Vertex
concepts.short_definition: A fundamental unit of a graph, representing an entity or point.

concepts.name: Cycle
concepts.short_definition: A path in a graph that starts and ends at the same vertex, and visits other vertices in between.

concepts.name: Path
concepts.short_definition: A sequence of vertices in a graph such that each adjacent pair of vertices is connected by an edge.

concepts.name: Disjoint Set Initialization
concepts.short_definition: The process of creating the initial state of the DSU, where each element is in its own set.

concepts.name: Element
concepts.short_definition: A single entity that can be part of a disjoint set.

concepts.name: Set Representative
concepts.short_definition: The unique identifier for a particular disjoint set, typically the root of its tree.

concepts.name: Forest
concepts.short_definition: A collection of disjoint trees, representing the state of the DSU.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: The amount of time an algorithm takes to run as a function of the input size.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: The amount of memory an algorithm uses as a function of the input size.

concepts.name: Applications of DSU
concepts.short_definition: Real-world problems and algorithms where the Disjoint Set Union data structure is effectively used.

concepts.name: Union by Height Optimization
concepts.short_definition: Synonym for Union by Rank.

concepts.name: Union by Size Optimization
concepts.short_definition: Synonym for Union by Size.

concepts.name: Flattening the Tree
concepts.short_definition: The process achieved by path compression, making tree structures more shallow.

concepts.name: Graph Connectivity
concepts.short_definition: The property of a graph that describes how well its vertices are connected.

concepts.name: Edge List
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph where edges are stored in a list.

concepts.name: Disjoint Set Operations
concepts.short_definition: The fundamental actions performed on a Disjoint Set Union data structure: initialization, find, and union.

concepts.name: Data Structure
concepts.short_definition: A particular way of organizing and storing data in a computer so that it can be accessed and modified efficiently.

concepts.name: Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: A step-by-step procedure or set of rules to be followed in calculations or other problem-solving operations.
